stem: The number of zebras in a population in 2020 was 1.45 times the number of zebras in this population in 2016. If the number of zebras in this population in 2016 is p% of the number of zebras in this population in 2020, what is the value of p, to the nearest whole number?
rationale: The correct answer is 69. Let x be the count in 2016 and y the count in 2020. Then y = 1.45x, so x/y = 1/1.45 ≈ 0.6897. Therefore x is about 68.97% of y, which rounds to 69%.
